definitions.text: At or to the back or far side of.
examples: The children were hiding behind the wall.
Behind the garage needs clearing asap.

definitions.text: Concealed by (something serving as a facade or disguise).
examples: Behind the smile was a cruel intention.

definitions.text: In the past, from the viewpoint of.
examples: All my problems are behind me.

definitions.text: At or in the rear or back part of something.
examples: The water flows out through the pipe, and the sediment collects behind.
We ran and ran, without once looking behind.

definitions.text: So as to come after someone or something in position, distance, advancement, ranking, time, etc.
examples: The slower runners were left a long way behind.
In the cricket match, England finished a long way behind.

definitions.text: Not advanced to the required or expected degree; overdue or in arrears.
examples: I'm very behind in my schedule.
My employer is two paychecks behind on paying my salary.
My watch is behind by four minutes.
This part fits under the behind of the vehicle.
She sneaked up on me from behind.
I was stung on the behind by a wasp.

definitions.text: Synonym of ass (“synecdochic: a person or their body”).
examples: Get your lazy behind out of bed!
